task.name: Web Research
task.description: Apply rigorous research standards for data integrity, source evaluation, and citation
task.objective: Conduct web research that meets rigorous data integrity, source evaluation, and citation standards. Every claim must be verified, every source evaluated, every citation formatted correctly.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
NEVER present information without verified sources — web search is REQUIRED for factual claims.
NEVER fabricate, estimate, or extrapolate data — state "DATA NOT FOUND IN SOURCES" if unavailable.
Sources with TS less than 6 are FORBIDDEN — do not use them.

substep.title: Read Research Standards
substep.action: Internalize all rules: data integrity, quantification, source evaluation, citation format.

substep.title: Execute Web Searches
substep.action: Use web search tools to find information on the research topic.
Gather multiple sources — critical claims require 2+ independent sources.

substep.title: Evaluate Each Source
substep.action: Score each source: Authority (AT), Trustability (TR), Topic Match (TM).
Calculate Total Score (TS) as average. Discard sources with TS less than 6.
Apply marketing language penalty to TR score where applicable.

substep.title: Group Domain Sources
substep.action: Multiple links from same domain count as ONE source unless they meet exception criteria.
Nest sub-sources under parent; each nested entry has its own evaluation.

substep.title: Verify Data Integrity
substep.action: Every number, statistic, or claim must cite its source inline (e.g., "[Source 12]").
State exact years/dates — never use vague terms like "recently" without defining period.

substep.title: Apply Quantification Standards
substep.action: Use explicit units: $ billions for market sizes, % for shares, % CAGR for growth rates.

substep.title: Distinguish Fact from Analysis
substep.action: Facts: directly from verified sources (cite with URL).
Analysis: your interpretation (state explicitly as analysis).

substep.title: Include Legend
substep.action: Add legend: "TS = Total Score (average of AT, TR, TM) | AT = Authority | TR = Trustability | TM = Topic Match | Scale: 1-10 | Threshold: TS ≥ 6"

substep.title: Format Citations
substep.action: Use format: [n] Title — URL — Research Date (YYYY-MM-DD) — Source Date — TS:x (AT:x TR:x TM:x)

substep.title: Document Discarded Sources
substep.action: List sources with TS less than 6 in separate "Sources Discarded" section with reason.

halt-conditions.condition: No sources found with TS 6 or higher — report and request guidance.
Critical claims cannot be verified with 2+ sources — flag explicitly.
